mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with funds rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, consumer spending growth decelerates sharply, reducing corporate revenue expectations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery, which amplifies earnings growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive mean return over 20 days in backtests, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rapid increases in industrial production indicate capacity utilization improvements that boost short-term corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 60 percent or more of post-shock recovery periods with rising industrial production, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.5 percent, borrowing costs for corporations and households increase sufficiently to suppress investment and consumption, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 20-day returns in over 50 percent of triggered instances, or the threshold effect is indistinguishable from a linear relationship in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total employment rises while the unemployment rate falls indicates robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal shows a negative or zero mean return over 60 days, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion regimes, elevated consumer price indices above 200 trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of late-cycle periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or the inflation threshold fails to predict return direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real GDP growth signal weakening economic momentum that immediately reduces near-term earnings forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of quarters with declining GDPC1, or the signal information coefficient remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.
